actionable_feedback: Untruncate and publish the full covariance_tuning_report.md so reviewers can see the entire Final Decision Table and validation content.

Add an explicit 'Final Decision Table' section at the top-level of the report that maps each recommendation to the exact control-packet row and value and labels any safety-critical items (e.g., 'safety_critical').
Provide a structured validation_checks_summary.json alongside the report containing pass/fail entries, test scenarios, and +/-5% sensitivity checks for safety-critical thresholds.
Include a machine-readable intermediate_calculations.csv (or JSON) that lists indexed inputs and derived velocities/accelerations/jerks for reproducibility.
Document the rubric item weights or the precise aggregation formula used to compute final_score (to resolve score-consistency questions).
Mark assumed engineering margins (e.g., the 20% margin) explicitly as 'ASSUMED / needs_review' in the Assumptions and Exceptions block and in the Final Decision Table.
Request a human domain-expert review for the final approval of covariance matrices and safety-related recommendations before public release.
artifact_specific_comments: Add 'Final Decision Table' as a clearly labeled, top-level heading in covariance_tuning_report.md mapping each recommendation to exact control-packet rows/values.
Embed or attach intermediate_calculations.csv with explicit indices (e.g., position[0], time[0]) to enable automated re-checks.
Provide validation_checks_summary.json that enumerates checks, expected ranges, pass/fail, and +/-5% sensitivity scenarios.
If conservative margins are used (20%), mark them 'ASSUMED / needs_review' in the Assumptions and Exceptions section.
Publish the aggregation weights/formula used to compute final_score so that score provenance is auditable.
feedback_summary: The baseline deliverable is high-quality: the covariance_tuning_report.md is present, calculations recompute correctly from the supplied control packet, and missing inputs are flagged. The grader and verifier both passed the artifact-contract checks and produced a strong final score. Two minor issues limit full semantic verification and traceability: (1) the artifact preview is truncated (full artifact access is required for complete review), and (2) the report omits an explicit, clearly labeled Final Decision Table and a separate structured validation_checks_summary.json (and the aggregation/weighting method used to compute the reported final_score is not published). Addressing these will make the deliverable fully machine-auditable and easier for a human domain expert to validate for safety-critical use.

raw_llm_output.suggested_revision: Produce a revised attempt that (a) publishes the untruncated full report, (b) adds a top-level, labeled Final Decision Table mapping each recommendation to control-packet fields and marking safety_critical items, (c) provides intermediate_calculations.csv for machine checks, (d) includes validation_checks_summary.json with test scenarios and sensitivity cases, and (e) documents rubric aggregation weights and flags assumed margins as 'ASSUMED / needs_review'. trace_specific_comments: Trace node script reproduces the numeric claims (segment velocities [0.4, 0.5, 0.2], acceleration changes [0.1, -0.3]). Include the script or a link to it in the artifact (e.g., reproducibility_script.js) so reviewers can re-run locally.
The trace shows deterministic precheck and computations; publishing the full, untruncated artifact will close the remaining verification gap.
